title: Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious

keywords: MOPC Journalist of the Year Law
primary_topics: MOPC naming CJ 'Journalist of the Year â€” Law' at Oct 25, 2023 Fairmont Hotel ceremony â€” first such MOPC awards in 78 years
Tribute to three 'Mega Media Movers' â€” MOPC chair Antonio 'Tony' Lopez, retired Inquirer chair Marixi R. Prieto, GMA chair/CEO Felipe 'Henry' L. Gozon
Origin story of CJ's 17-year Inquirer column career â€” Marixi's recruitment over Dr. Salonga's objection
Tribute to Henry Gozon as foremost aviation-law expert turned GMA Network chairman
sub_topics: MOPC citation: 'prolific work and prodigious excellence in writing about law and justice with depth, clarity, and command of language, thereby strengthening the people's faith in the third and often misunderstood branch of government, the judiciary'
Citation error noted by CJ â€” '12 [should be 14] books on law, faith, and the Supreme Court'
CJ's self-description as 'accidental writer; in short, tsamba lang 'yon'

'Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious' as CJ's witty reaction word â€” 'typical of how lawyers, like me, tend to be highfalutin, muddled, and long-worded' vs. journalists' clear, simple writing he tries to imitate

Italian bell trophies: 15 inches tall, three kilos, ordered in Italy ('home to the world's oldest and best bell-manufacturing technology, dating back a thousand years') â€” symbolize freedom, excellence, achievement, glory, perfection of craft
Inquirer 'Business Writer of the Year' co-honoree Dax Lucas at the same ceremony

Marixi origin story â€” late 1980s newsprint sales to Inquirer; Inquirer a 'giant in circulation but a dwarf in money'; her P10 million loan convertible to common stock at her option; two-bloc deadlock resolved by assigning holdings to her â€” Inquirer 'magically fell on her lap as the ACCIDENTAL OWNER'

Marixi vs Dr. Jovito R. Salonga exchange on the columnist invitation â€” Salonga: 'I do not know of any CJ who became a columnist. Chief justices do not write history; they make it'; Marixi: 'I do not know of any chief justice who had been offered to write a regular column'

17 years of weekly Inquirer columns (compromise from Marixi's thrice-weekly request) â€” without fail, without vacation, despite COVID Dec 2022, despite Leni's death April 9 (eulogy became most-visited Inquirer.net column as of August 2023), despite travel (Kuala Lumpur speech)
Longer at the Inquirer (17 years) than at the SC (11 years)
WDR1 Amazon bestseller (3rd, courts category) â€” bested only by Jeffrey Toobin (CNN) and Antonin Scalia (US SC)
Royalties donated to Inquirer Newsboy Foundation
Henry Gozon as foremost aviation-law expert â€” counsel of Philippine Airlines vs CJ as counsel of KLM and Continental ('clashed passionately but socialized privately')
CJ repeatedly asked Henry to join the SC as an incumbent justice; Henry declined
Dec 6, 2006 â€” CJ's SC retirement; Henry and GMA president Jimmy Duavit visited home, invited him to GMA board of directors
GMA Nielsen dominance â€” rating higher than the 10 other major networks combined; P7.5B+ 2021 net profit (highest in any network's history)
Closing self-deprecation: 'honorary Ph.D. from the MOPC University of Journalism'

events: Oct 25, 2023 â€” MOPC awards ceremony at Fairmont Hotel Makati
Late 1980s â€” Marixi's first contact with Inquirer via newsprint sales
Inquirer ownership consolidation â€” Marixi as 'accidental owner'

stances.claim: CJ owes the MOPC 'Journalist of the Year â€” Law' recognition to three 'Mega Media Movers' â€” MOPC chair Antonio 'Tony' Lopez, retired Inquirer chair Marixi R. Prieto, and GMA Network chair/CEO Felipe 'Henry' L. Gozon â€” none of whose contributions are interchangeable.

CJ is properly described not as a 'Journalist' (a title reserved for 'battle-tested stars of the journalism profession') but as an 'accidental writer' â€” 'tsamba lang 'yon' â€” and 'Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious' is the right reaction word because it exemplifies how 'lawyers, like me, tend to be highfalutin, muddled, and long-worded,' in contrast to the clear, simple writing of journalists that he tries to imitate.
stances.rhetorical_move: self-deprecating-acceptance with lawyer-vs-journalist contrast as title-deflection

stances.claim: Marixi R. Prieto became the Inquirer's 'accidental owner' â€” in the late 1980s, while selling newsprint to an Inquirer that was 'a giant in circulation but a dwarf in money,' she lent it P10 million convertible to common stock at her option; the two equal blocs that owned the paper compromised their control deadlock by assigning their holdings to her, and the Inquirer 'magically fell on her lap.'
stances.rhetorical_move: accidental-ownership origin narrative paralleling CJ's 'accidental writer' self-description

stances.claim: Marixi won the columnist-recruitment 'battle of wits' against Dr. Salonga's objection â€” Salonga: 'I do not know of any CJ who became a columnist. Chief justices do not write history; they make it'; Marixi: 'I do not know of any chief justice who had been offered to write a regular column' â€” resulting in CJ's compromise to write weekly (not thrice weekly), without fail, without vacation, despite illnesses (COVID Dec 2022), family death (Leni April 9, 2023), and travel (Kuala Lumpur speech) â€” making him an Inquirer columnist for 17 years, longer than his 11 years on the SC.

Henry Gozon is 'our foremost expert on aviation law' â€” having opposed CJ in clashes between Philippine Airlines (Gozon's client) and KLM/Continental (CJ's clients) 50 years ago, 'clashed passionately but socialized privately' â€” and CJ repeatedly invited him to the SC, which Henry declined; CJ later 'became a witness to why he declined membership in the Court' as Henry built GMA into 'the largest, most trusted, and best TV-radio network' with Nielsen ratings higher than the 10 other major networks combined and a record P7.5B+ 2021 net profit, all under the motto 'Sa totoo lang tayo.'

notable_anecdotes.summary: Tony Lopez's text + CJ's reply exchange: Tony stressed it was the first MOPC awards in 78 years; CJ replied 'I am not worthy of the appellation Journalist... At best, I am just an ACCIDENTAL WRITER; in short, tsamba lang 'yon.' When Tony asked what he thought of the citation, CJ replied 'Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious!' â€” the column's title and a self-aware critique of lawyerly prose.

The Italian bell trophies â€” 15 inches tall, three kilos, sourced from Italy 'home to the world's oldest and best bell-manufacturing technology, dating back a thousand years' â€” symbolizing 'freedom, excellence, achievement, glory, and perfection of craft.' A craftsmanship-detail flourish CJ rarely indulges in.

Marixi's accidental ownership of the Inquirer: lending P10M convertible to common stock to a 'giant in circulation but a dwarf in money,' then receiving both blocs' holdings as a deadlock compromise â€” 'the Inquirer magically fell on her lap as the ACCIDENTAL OWNER.' A clean parallel to CJ's own 'accidental writer' self-description that structures the column's twin themes.
The Salonga-vs-Marixi 'battle of wits' â€” verbatim quotes â€” repeated from earlier columns (CC09) but here landing at higher rhetorical compression and with Marixi's victory explicitly declared.

Leni's eulogy column becoming 'the most visited column in the Inquirer.net as of August 2023' â€” a quiet posthumous tribute embedded in a different column's anecdote about CJ's commitment to never missing a Sunday.
The 50-year aviation-law rivalry: Gozon (PAL) vs Panganiban (KLM, Continental) â€” 'clashed passionately but socialized privately' â€” a six-word capsule of professional decorum.
Dec 6, 2006 SC 'graduation' immediately followed by the Gozon-Duavit home visit recruiting CJ to the GMA board â€” a 24-hour transition between branches of public life.

GMA's competitive vindication: P7.5B+ 2021 net profit 'the highest in the history of any network,' with Nielsen ratings 'higher than those of the 10 other major networks combined,' under the motto 'Sa totoo lang tayo' â€” ABS-CBN closure tacitly mentioned as context.
Closing 'honorary Ph.D. from the MOPC University of Journalism' â€” CJ's signature self-deprecating-but-pleased acceptance device.

decision_framework_signals: uses twin-accidental motif ('accidental writer' + 'accidental owner') to structure the tribute and reframe achievement as serendipity
treats self-deprecating-acceptance as proper register for honors received without prior knowledge
deploys lawyer-vs-journalist prose contrast as self-critique and standard-of-aspiration
uses persistence-evidence (17 years without fail despite illness, death, travel) to qualify the 'accidental writer' label as still-meritorious
treats professional rivalry resolved by socializing privately as a model of decorum
uses Nielsen + net-profit metrics as vindication-by-results for Gozon's choice to decline the SC
applies parenthetical-correction within citation as graceful factual-accuracy device

one_paragraph_summary: Panganiban accepts the Manila Overseas Press Club's 'Journalist of the Year â€” Law' award at the Oct 25, 2023 Fairmont Hotel Makati ceremony â€” the first such MOPC awards in 78 years â€” with the citation honoring his 'prolific work and prodigious excellence in writing about law and justice with depth, clarity, and command of language, thereby strengthening the people's faith in the third and often misunderstood branch of government, the judiciary' (and noting in passing that the cited '12 books' should be 14). He attributes the recognition to three 'Mega Media Movers': MOPC chair Antonio 'Tony' Lopez, retired Inquirer chair Marixi R. Prieto, and GMA Network chair/CEO Felipe 'Henry' L. Gozon. He declines the title 'Journalist' (reserved for 'battle-tested stars of the journalism profession') and self-describes as 'ACCIDENTAL WRITER â€” tsamba lang 'yon'; when Tony asked what he thought of the citation, CJ replied 'Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious!' â€” the column's title and a self-aware critique that lawyers 'tend to be highfalutin, muddled, and long-worded' while journalists write clearly and simply, which he tries to imitate. He admires the Italian bell trophies (15 inches, three kilos, from a thousand-year-old bell-making tradition) and the co-honor of Inquirer's Dax Lucas as Business Writer of the Year. He recounts Marixi's late-1980s entry into the Inquirer as newsprint seller to 'a giant in circulation but a dwarf in money,' her P10 million convertible loan, and the two-bloc deadlock compromise that made her the 'ACCIDENTAL OWNER' â€” paralleling his own 'accidental writer' description. After his 2006 retirement Marixi invited him to write thrice weekly; Dr. Jovito R. Salonga objected with 'I do not know of any CJ who became a columnist. Chief justices do not write history; they make it'; Marixi parried 'I do not know of any chief justice who had been offered to write a regular column' and 'won the battle of wits,' leading to CJ's 17-year weekly run (longer than his 11 years on the SC) â€” without fail, without vacation, despite COVID in December 2022, despite Leni's unexpected death on April 9, 2023 (whose eulogy column became Inquirer.net's most-visited as of August 2023), and despite international travel. The first WDR volume in 2011 placed third in Amazon's courts-category bestseller list (bested only by Jeffrey Toobin of CNN and Justice Antonin Scalia of the US Supreme Court); CJ donated royalties to the Inquirer Newsboy Foundation. He then turns to Henry Gozon as 'our foremost expert on aviation law,' recalling their 50-year-ago clashes between Gozon as Philippine Airlines counsel and CJ as KLM and Continental counsel â€” 'clashed passionately but socialized privately.' CJ repeatedly invited Gozon to the SC during his incumbency; Gozon declined. On Dec 6, 2006 â€” CJ's day of SC 'graduation' â€” Gozon and GMA president Jimmy Duavit visited him at home to invite him to the GMA board, and CJ 'became a witness to why he declined' as Gozon built GMA into 'the largest, most trusted, and best TV-radio network long before ABS-CBN was closed' â€” today unrivaled per Nielsen with ratings higher than the 10 other major networks combined and a record P7.5B+ 2021 net profit, under the motto 'Sa totoo lang tayo.' He closes: 'I feel like receiving an honorary Ph.D. from the MOPC University of Journalism.'
